resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived MCM6/LCT rs4988235 CT call is available for lactase-persistence context. This is not a diagnosis, symptom explanation, food-sensitivity result, diet recommendation, or supplement recommendation.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This marker can provide lactose-related context, but symptoms, diet history, labs, environment, medications, age, and professional review matter for nutrition decisions.

healthyNutritionContext: Use this report as educational nutrition-genetics context, not as a meal plan, supplement plan, food-restriction list, lab interpretation, or child-specific nutrition guide.

Treat GeneInformed sample food-reaction, vitamin, mineral, omega-3, genetic-risk, population-share, and advice text as observed sample-report structure unless validated local evidence supports equivalent sections.
Discuss symptoms, diet history, labs, medications, age, children, and health goals with qualified nutrition or clinical professionals.
Do not change foods, supplements, tests, or treatment based on genotype alone.

plainEnglishLimits: Nutrition and nutrient status depend on diet, symptoms, medications, labs, medical history, environment, age, and professional review, not genotype alone.
Public sample rows show observed report format and source binding; they are not local personal findings unless the local run supplies equivalent validated evidence.

The local fixture does not include diet history, food-reaction model output, vitamin blood levels, mineral blood levels, fatty-acid labs, child-specific context, medication review, or a calibrated nutrition model.

No calibrated healthy-nutrition, food-reaction, vitamin, mineral, omega-3, meal-plan, child-specific nutrition, supplement, symptom, diet-history, lab, metabolic, or ancestry applicability model is supplied with this fixture.
The GeneInformed Healthy Nutrition rows are public sample-report examples and are not local target recommendations.

Broader symptoms, food history, diet history, age, children, activity history, labs, medications, medical history, ancestry applicability, environment, and professional assessment are not represented in this fixture.
Visible sample gene rows do not include rsIDs or genomic coordinates, so they are preserved as sample display rows instead of resolved biological variants.
uncertainty: Nutrition, food response, nutrient status, symptoms, labs, glucose, weight, supplement response, and metabolic outcomes depend on genetics plus non-genetic context and professional review.
Public sample report rows are useful for output-format parity and citation binding, but they do not establish local genotype effects or individual recommendations.
A single marker should not be used to diagnose conditions, explain symptoms, prescribe diets, recommend supplements, interpret labs, create child-specific guidance, or guide treatment.
limitations: This validation result uses derived evidence only; raw genome data is not included.

The report is educational and does not create meal plans, create child-specific nutrition guidance, prescribe diet changes, recommend supplements, diagnose symptoms, infer metabolic disease, interpret labs, or provide treatment guidance.
Healthy Nutrition sample rows are source-bound to the public PDF for format parity; marketplace detail parity remains pending.
